pages.text: $(o)"Ascension is not a one-way climb â€” every step demands a price."$()$(br2)The $(l)Vow System$() is the spine of Ascension: each stage (1-4) can bind $(l)one vow$() that grants a benefit and exacts a cost.

pages.title: General Rules
pages.text: Â· One vow per stage$(br)Â· Your ascension stage must be $(l)â‰Ą$() the vow's required stage$(br)Â· Once bound, the vow merges into your $(l)total stat block$()$(br)Â· Commands can rebind freely (cost-free version)$(br2)$(o)"A vow is no contract â€” it is what you said to yourself. Easy to break; hard to forget."$()

pages.title: Choosing & Switching
pages.text: Manage your vows via commands:$(br2)$(l)/transcend_vow list$()$(br)View all four bound vows$(br2)$(l)/transcend_vow available <stage>$()$(br)List selectable vows for a stage$(br2)$(l)/transcend_vow set <stage> <vowId>$()$(br)Bind one$(br2)$(l)/transcend_vow clear <stage>$()$(br)Unbind a stage's vow

pages.title: On the Nature of Vows
pages.text: In the old civilization, vows were never "seals imposed by a priest" â€” they were $(l)agreements you struck with one face of yourself$().$(br2)To choose Focus is to admit: "I will walk only one road."$(br)To choose Leyline Sovereign is to admit: "I will trade my temperance for more circles."$(br2)$(o)"A vow does not make you stronger â€” it only makes your strength legible."$() â€” last lesson of the Ascension mentor
